name: Jálama Peak Circular Trail
name_original: Subida al Jálama – trail run circular
description: Tackle the Ascent to Jalama circular trail run, a difficult 6.2-mile route in Sierra de Gata with 1402 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging around San Martín De Trevejo offers routes through varied terrain, from challenging mountain paths to more gentle loops. The region features elevations that provide both demanding climbs and scenic vistas. Running routes here often traverse natural landscapes, including areas with significant elevation changes. The network of jogging routes around San Martín De Trevejo caters to different fitness levels, with options ranging from moderate to difficult. Best jogging routes around San Martín De Trevejo The most popular jogging route is Jálama Peak Circular Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Hermitage of the Holy Spirit – Eljas loop from Valverde del Fresno, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain, connecting local points of interest. Local runners also love the Running loop from Valverde del Fresno, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 49 minutes. Jogging around San Martín De Trevejo is defined by hilly terrain, moderate ascents, and scenic loops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate running routes to more challenging trails. The routes in San Martín De Trevejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore San Martín De Trevejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around San Martín De Trevejo?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around San Martín De Trevejo, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. The komoot community has explored 13 routes in total.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in San Martín De Trevejo?
A: The routes around San Martín De Trevejo are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no routes specifically marked as easy. However, some moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Valverde del Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 miles (7.9 km) with moderate elevation, might be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in San Martín De Trevejo?
A: Jogging around San Martín De Trevejo is characterized by hilly terrain, moderate ascents, and scenic loops. You'll encounter varied landscapes, often with significant elevation changes, providing both demanding climbs and rewarding vistas.

Q: What are some popular running routes in the region?
A: While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990944752/jalama-peak-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jálama Peak Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is very popular for its challenging nature and panoramic views, other favorites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit – Eljas loop from Valverde del Fresno&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a mix of terrain and connects local points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around San Martín De Trevejo are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990944752/jalama-peak-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jálama Peak Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32158133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit – Eljas loop from Eljas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the jogging routes around San Martín De Trevejo?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. For instance, the area features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4958182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jálama Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic vistas. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3555793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in San Martín De Trevejo?
A: The running routes in San Martín De Trevejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the rewarding scenic views found along the trails.

Q: What is the typical distance for a running route in this area?
A: The running routes vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Valverde del Fresno&lt;/a&gt; is about 4.9 miles (7.9 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990944752/jalama-peak-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jálama Peak Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; extends to 6.2 miles (10.0 km). You can find options ranging from moderate distances to longer, more challenging runs.

Q: Are there any routes that start from Eljas?
A: Yes, you can find routes starting from Eljas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32158133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit – Eljas loop from Eljas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9 miles (11.2 km) route that begins in Eljas and offers a good running experience.

Q: Can I find running routes that connect different local towns?
A: Yes, some routes connect local points of interest and towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17314351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit – Eljas loop from Valverde del Fresno&lt;/a&gt; links Valverde del Fresno with other areas, providing a diverse running experience.

Q: How challenging are the running trails in San Martín De Trevejo?
A: The running trails in San Martín De Trevejo range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 8 moderate routes and 5 difficult routes, indicating that many trails involve significant elevation changes and require a good level of fitness.
tips:
At 1,487 metres above sea level, it is one of the highest peaks in the Sierra de Gata.
The route to climb Pico Jálama is popular with hikers and offers spectacular views of the region. The route begins in the town of Acebo and passes by the Mirador de La Ventosa and Puerto de Perales.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Ascent to Jalama - circular trail run?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for jogging this trail is approximately 1 hour and 28 minutes. This is a challenging route, so actual times may vary based on your fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to Jalama - circular trail run a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a circular trail run, meaning it starts and ends at the same point, offering a varied experience without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail run?
faqs.answer: The trail is characterized by significant elevation changes, leading up to the Pico Jálama Summit. You can expect varied terrain typical of mountain trails in Sierra de Gata, likely including rocky paths, dirt tracks, and potentially some steeper sections. It's classified as a difficult trail run.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this route is the Pico Jálama Summit. From the summit, you can expect expansive views of the surrounding Sierra de Gata landscape. The trail also offers scenic mountain vistas throughout.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta A Fala and the Subida al Jálama trails, offering a blend of different paths within the Sierra de Gata region.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking details are not provided, trails in Sierra de Gata typically have designated parking areas near common access points. Look for parking near the start of the circular route, often in or near a local village or a marked recreational area.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to Jalama - circular trail run dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in Sierra de Gata, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Be prepared to carry out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for trail running in Sierra de Gata. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures or even snow at higher elevations like Pico Jálama.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or Sierra de Gata Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for general access to trails within the Sierra de Gata Natural Park. However, always check local regulations before your visit, as rules can change, especially for protected areas.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult trail run?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, you should bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, energy snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather.

faqs.question: Are there any advanced trail running routes near Jalama?
faqs.answer: The Ascent to Jalama - circular trail run itself is considered difficult. The Sierra de Gata region offers numerous challenging mountain trails, and combining sections of trails like Ruta A Fala or other routes to Pico Jálama can create even more advanced options for experienced runners.
